strategy_notes: This iteration is correctness-focused only. The previous attempt failed because the semantic fix was incomplete or had encoding bugs. Query Optimizer should: (1) Load metadata.txt to get row count and column encoding info (critical — iteration 1 may have guessed row counts). (2) Map each binary column file with proper type (int32_t for quantity/discount/shipdate, int64_t for extendedprice). (3) Carefully implement predicate evaluation — discount encoding may use fixed-point (x/100) or raw ints; shipdate uses days since epoch; understand what encoding.json specifies. (4) Execute aggregation with proper type conversion: multiply int64_t * int32_t, then divide by 10000 for decimal precision. (5) Apply mmap hints (MADV_SEQUENTIAL) for efficient I/O. Do NOT attempt performance optimizations (parallelism, SIMD, zone maps) until correctness is verified. Once binary storage is working and result matches ground truth, remaining iteration can add parallelism + zone maps for final speedup.
